volumeTitle: The Little Carthaginian. Pseudolus. The Rope
label: The Little Carthaginian (Section 1.2.404b - Section 2.1.476b)

sections.label: Section 1.2.404b
sections.translation: You are not ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.404c
sections.translation: I am not .

sections.label: Section 1.2.404d
sections.translation: That I may believe you , give me a kiss then .

sections.label: Section 1.2.405
sections.original: Mox dabo , quom ab re divina rediero .
sections.translation: I ’ ll give you one by-and-by , when I return from the sacrifice .

sections.label: Section 1.2.405b
sections.translation: Be off , then , in all haste .

sections.label: Section 1.2.406
sections.translation: Follow me , sister .

sections.label: Section 1.2.406b
sections.translation: And do you hear too ? Pay all compliments to Venus in my name .

sections.label: Section 1.2.407b
sections.translation: I ’ ll pay them .

sections.label: Section 1.2.407c
sections.translation: Listen to this , too —

sections.label: Section 1.2.407d
sections.translation: What is it ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.408
sections.original: Paucis verbis rem divinam facito . atque audin ? respice .
sections.translation: Perform the ceremony in few words . And do you hear ? Look back at me . ( She looks back .) She did look back . By my troth , I trust that Venus will do the same for you .

sections.label: Section 1.3.410
sections.original: quid nunc mi es auctor , Milphio ?
sections.translation: What now do you advise me to do , Milphio ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.410b
sections.translation: To give me a beating , and then have an auction [*] ; for ( pointing to the house ) really , upon my faith , with utter impunity you might put up this house for sale .

sections.label: Section 1.3.413
sections.translation: Why so ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.413b
sections.original: Maiorem partem in ore habitas meo .
sections.translation: For the greater part you make your dwelling in my mouth [*] .

sections.label: Section 1.3.414
sections.translation: Do have done with those expressions .

sections.label: Section 1.3.414b
sections.translation: What now do you wish ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.415
sections.translation: I just now gave three hundred Philippeans to the bailiff Collybiscus , before you called me out of doors . I now adjure you , Milphio , by this right hand , and by this left hand its sister , and by your eyes , and by my passion , and by my own Adelphasium ,

sections.label: Section 1.3.420
sections.translation: and by your liberty [*] —

sections.label: Section 1.3.420b
sections.translation: Why , now you adjure me by nothing at all .

sections.label: Section 1.3.421
sections.original: Mi Milphidisce , mea commoditas , mea salus ,
sections.translation: My dear little Milphio , my kind occasion , my safeguard , do what you promised me you would do , that I may prove the ruin of this Procurer .

sections.label: Section 1.3.423b
sections.translation: Why , that ’ s very easy to be done . Be off , bring here with you your witnesses ; meanwhile , in-doors I ’ ll forthwith provide your bailiff with my disguise and stratagems . Make haste and be off .

sections.label: Section 1.3.427
sections.translation: I fly .

sections.label: Section 1.3.427b
sections.original: Meum est ístuc magis officium quam tuom .
sections.translation: That ’ s more my part [*] than yours .

sections.label: Section 1.3.428
sections.original: Egone egone , si istuc lepide ecfexis —
sections.translation: Should I not , should I not , if you effect this adroitly [*] —

sections.label: Section 1.3.428b
sections.translation: Only do begone .

sections.label: Section 1.3.429
sections.translation: Ought I not this very day —

sections.label: Section 1.3.429b
sections.translation: Only do be off .

sections.label: Section 1.3.429c
sections.translation: To give you freedom

sections.label: Section 1.3.430
sections.translation: Only do begone .

sections.label: Section 1.3.430b
sections.translation: By my troth , I should not deserve —

sections.label: Section 1.3.430c
sections.translation: ah !

sections.label: Section 1.3.430d
sections.translation: Bah !

sections.label: Section 1.3.430e
sections.translation: Only do be off .

sections.label: Section 1.3.431
sections.translation: As many as are the dead in Acheron —

sections.label: Section 1.3.431b
sections.translation: Will you , then , move off ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.432
sections.translation: Nor yet as many as there are waves in the sea —

sections.label: Section 1.3.432b
sections.translation: Are you going to move off ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.433
sections.translation: Nor as many as there are clouds —

sections.label: Section 1.3.433b
sections.translation: Do you persist in going on this way ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.434
sections.translation: Nor as there are stars in heaven —

sections.label: Section 1.3.434b
sections.translation: Do you persist in dinning my ears ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.435
sections.original: Neque hoc neque illud neque — enim vero serio —
sections.translation: Neither this thing nor that ; nor yet , indeed , seriously speaking — nor , by my faith , indeed . What need is there of words ? And why not ?— a thing that in one word — here we may say anything we please — and yet , i ’ faith , not seriously in reality . D ’ ye see how ’ tis ? So may the Gods bless me !— do you wish me to tell you in honest truth ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.440
sections.original: quod hic inter nos liceat ? ita me Iuppiter ,
sections.translation: A thing that here we may between ourselves — so help me Jupiter — Do you see how ? Look you — do you believe what I tell you ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.442
sections.original: Si nequeo facere ut abeas , egomet abiero ;
sections.translation: If I cannot make you go away , I shall go away myself : for really , upon my faith , there ’ s need of an Oedipus [*] as a diviner for this speech of yours , him who was the interpreter to the Sphinx .

sections.label: Section 1.3.445
sections.original: Illic hínc iratus abiit . nunc mihi cautio est ,
sections.translation: He has gone off in a passion ; now must I beware , lest , through my own fault , I place an impediment in the way of my love . I ’ ll go and fetch the witnesses , since love commands me , a free man , to be obedient to my own slave .

sections.label: Section 2.1.449
sections.original: Di illum infelicent omnes , qui post hunc diem
sections.translation: May all the Gods render him unfortunate , should any Procurer , after this day , ever immolate any victim to Venus , or should any one sacrifice a single grain of frankincense .

sections.label: Section 2.1.452
sections.original: nam ego hodie infelix dis meis iratissumis
sections.translation: For wretched I , this day , have sacrificed to my most wrathful Deities [*] six lambs , and still I could not manage to make Venus to be propitious unto me .

sections.label: Section 2.1.455
sections.original: quoniam litare nequeo , ábii illim ilico
sections.translation: Since I could not appease her , forthwith I departed thence in a passion ; I forbade the entrails to be cut , and would not examine them . Inasmuch as the soothsayer pronounced them not propitious , I deemed the Goddess not deserving . By these means I fairly played a trick upon the greedy Venus .

sections.label: Section 2.1.458
sections.original: quando id quod sat erat satis habere noluit ,
sections.translation: When , that which was enough , she would not have to be enough , I made a pause . ’ Tis thus I act , and thus it befits me to act .

sections.label: Section 2.1.460
sections.original: ego faxo posthac di deaeque ceteri
sections.translation: I ’ ll make the other Gods and Goddesses henceforth more contented , and less greedy , when they know how the Procurer put a trick upon Venus .

sections.label: Section 2.1.463
sections.translation: The soothsayer , in manner right worthy of him , a fellow not worth threepence , said that in all the entrails misfortune and loss were portended to me , and that the Gods were angry with me .

sections.label: Section 2.1.466
sections.original: quid ei divini aut humani aequomst credere ?
sections.translation: In what matter either divine or human is it right for me to put trust in him ? Just after that , a mina of silver was given me . But where , pray , has this Captain stopped just now , who gave it me , and whom I ’ ve invited to breakfast ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.470
sections.translation: But look ! here he comes .

sections.label: Section 2.1.470b
sections.translation: So , as I began to tell you , you sorry pimp , about that Pentethronic battle [*] , in which , with my own hands , in one day , I slew sixty thousand flying men .

sections.label: Section 2.1.474
sections.translation: Heyday ! Flying men ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.474b
sections.translation: Certainly I do affirm it .

sections.label: Section 2.1.475
sections.original: An , opsecro , usquam sunt homines volatici ?
sections.translation: Prithee , are there anywhere men that fly ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.476
sections.translation: There were ; but I slew them .

sections.label: Section 2.1.476b
sections.translation: How could you ?
